rulings.Case Title: R. R. VS. EL TRABAJO INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Edward Alkhasov'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set Number One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 22, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 9, Edward Alkhasov'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defendant'S Special Interrogatories, Set Number One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Denied. The motion was not timely filed. See Code of Civil Procedure Section 2030.300(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to brad@kaplans.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HAZEL WALSH VS. KINDRED HEALTHCARE INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Pltf Hazel Walsh;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 22, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, DEFENDANT KINDRED NURSING CENTERS WEST, LLC's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Hazel Walsh. Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to address the following issues: (1) What information does defendant seek to learn during plaintiff's deposition; (2) What is the status of defendant's subpoenas for plaintiff's medical records and what has plaintiff done to assist or hinder defendant's acquisition of these documents; (3) Is Marianne Cunningham an adequate deponent to testify on the issues to be covered by plaintiff's deposition; and (4) What is the status of defendant's efforts to depose Ms. Cunningham? =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DOUGLAS CHUCK, VS. KERWIN WAI TSE, AN INDIVIDUAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 22, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT DONNY KWOK LUNG AO IEONG, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND Granted, pursuant to CCP 473(b). Defendant moved within 3 months to set aside the default, and there is no showing that plaintiff will suffer any prejudice if the relief is granted. There has been no discovery conducted in the case. This is inexcusable neglect. rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 22, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANT BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Defendants agreed that San Francisco is the appropriate forum for litigating any disputes arising out of the lease. The Court has subject matter jurisidiction. Pacific Decision Sciences Corp. v. Superior Court (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1100 is not on point. Pacific Decision held that a Court exceeds its jurisdiction when it issues a turnover order for property outside the state. The holding in that case was based on strict construction of law pertaining to attachment, and is not applicable to whether the court in this case has subject matter jurisdiction. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
